skill.name: documentation
skill.description: Creates TSDoc comments, Storybook stories, VitePress pages, and TypeDoc API documentation for TypeScript and React projects.
skill.summary: Generate and maintain TypeScript and React documentation with TSDoc, Storybook, VitePress, and TypeDoc.
skill.author: CrazySwami

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples and prose substring matches. The skill documents local commands such as pnpm docs and Storybook tasks, with no evidence of malicious execution, network access, exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The flagged command patterns appear in Markdown code fences and a command table for local documentation tools. They do not execute by themselves and do not include dynamic input, network callbacks, or shell pipelines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown documentation examples or command names, not executable script files. The only real commands are local documentation commands without user-controlled interpolation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The high findings point to words such as description in prose or templates. No MD5, SHA1, DES API use, cryptographic code, or credential handling was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose labels and documentation templates, not cryptographic APIs. The detector appears to have matched weak algorithm names inside ordinary words.

content.value_statement: Teams lose context when TypeScript APIs, React components, and docs drift apart. This skill helps create TSDoc, Storybook, VitePress, and TypeDoc documentation.
actual_capabilities: Drafts TSDoc comments for exported functions, hooks, components, interfaces, and types.
Creates Storybook story patterns for React components with default, loading, and error states.
Guides VitePress documentation updates in docs/**/*.md files.
Supports TypeDoc API documentation generation through local project commands.
Provides a documentation checklist for TypeScript and React codebases.
limitations: Assumes the project already uses TypeScript, React, Storybook, VitePress, or TypeDoc.
Does not define custom documentation architecture beyond the listed tools.
Requires project-specific review for accurate examples and component behavior.
Mentions local pnpm commands but does not install missing dependencies.

use_cases.title: Document New Components
use_cases.description: Add TSDoc and Storybook coverage when a React component is ready for reuse.

use_cases.title: Refresh API Reference
use_cases.description: Update comments and regenerate TypeDoc pages after TypeScript API changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Documentation
use_cases.description: Create VitePress pages that explain usage patterns for internal or public developers.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic TSDoc
prompt_templates.prompt: Add clear TSDoc comments to the exported functions in this TypeScript file. Include parameters, returns, and one simple example.

prompt_templates.title: Create Component Stories
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Storybook stories for this React component. Include default, loading, and error states that match the component props.

prompt_templates.title: Update API Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changed TypeScript exports, improve their TSDoc, and identify which TypeDoc API pages should be regenerated.

prompt_templates.title: Build Documentation Coverage Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this TypeScript and React codebase for missing TSDoc, Storybook, and VitePress coverage. Prioritize the work by user impact.

output_examples.input: A utility function has no comments and confusing parameter names.
output_examples.output: A concise TSDoc summary with parameter descriptions, a return description, and a usage example written for developers.

output_examples.input: A React component needs Storybook coverage.
output: A default story that shows the normal state.
A loading story that documents pending behavior.
An error story that shows failure handling.

output_examples.input: A docs folder needs updates after API changes.
output_examples.output: A VitePress update plan that lists changed pages, sidebar updates, and the TypeDoc regeneration step.

best_practices: Keep examples short and focused on common developer workflows.
Document exported APIs when behavior, parameters, or return values are not obvious.
Regenerate API docs after TSDoc changes and review generated pages before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not add generic comments that repeat function or variable names.
Do not create Storybook stories that pass invalid props or impossible states.
Do not publish generated API docs without checking navigation and examples.

faq.question: What documentation systems does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets TSDoc, Storybook, VitePress, and TypeDoc for TypeScript and React projects.

faq.question: Can it document plain JavaScript projects?
faq.answer: It may help with general comments, but its guidance is written for TypeScript and React workflows.

faq.question: Does it run documentation commands automatically?
faq.answer: It lists local pnpm commands, but command execution depends on the host tool and user approval settings.

faq.question: Can it create Storybook stories from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides a standard Storybook pattern with default, loading, and error examples.

faq.question: Does it validate documentation accuracy?
faq.answer: It can review comments and examples, but humans should verify behavior against the real code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for marketplace publishing?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk because static alerts were documentation references, not executable malicious code.
